Q1.note: n_inf uses rho_Lambda_obs as input (sec 5.2 circularity). Q is dimensionally a rate but is *not* a photon-channel rate — axiom does not specify whether sink couples to em sector.

Q2.note: Reference value cited from Chluba 2016; not re-derived here.

Q3.interpretation_warning: If 1.02e-8 is the SQT *total*, it is BELOW the LCDM Silk-damping baseline (~2e-8) — implying a *negative* deviation. Sign of the falsifier is then opposite to what base.md sec 4.3 states. If it is *additional* on top of LCDM, total ~3e-8, still detectable, but base.md sec 4.3 does not specify which interpretation.

Q4.note: base.md sec 4.3 quotes 'foreground 2 sigma' which corresponds to ILC-class separation (mu/5e-9 ~ 2). Best-case multi-component separation gives ~10 sigma but is observatory-dependent.

Q5.reason: Branching depends on epoch of energy injection, which the SQT axioms (1-6) do not specify. Without a *time profile* of Q(z), y/mu is not predicted. Adding such a profile = extra input -> PASS_STRONG forfeit (PASS_IDENTITY at best).

verdict.recommended_status: PENDING (PASS_IDENTITY risk + Lambda circularity + photon-channel unspecified)
